plan: Create a `vercel.json` configuration file at the project root with: framework preset set to `vite`, build command `npm run build`, output directory `dist`, and a catch-all rewrite rule (`"source": "/(.*)", "destination": "/index.html"`) as a safety net for any non-hash route refreshes.
Run the full validation suite locally (`npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test`) to confirm the project builds and passes all checks before deployment.

Run `npm run build && npm run preview` locally and manually verify all five acceptance criteria against the preview server: landing page loads without console errors, browse page loads, random draw mode works, and hash-based routes survive refresh.

Connect the GitHub repository to Vercel (via `vercel` CLI or Vercel dashboard), configure the project with the matching build settings (build command: `npm run build`, output: `dist`, install: `npm install`), and trigger a production deployment.

Verify all acceptance criteria against the live production URL: (1) deployment succeeds, (2) client-side routes resolve after refresh, (3) no console errors on landing page, (4) browse page loads, (5) random draw mode works.
Add the production URL to the project README or CLAUDE.md if appropriate, and mark the ticket as passes=true.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` before deploying to catch any pre-existing issues.
After local build, serve with `npm run preview` and check each route (`/`, `/#/browse`, `/#/play`, `/#/play/draw-three`, `/#/guide`) — verify they all load and survive a hard refresh.
Open browser DevTools console on the production landing page and confirm zero errors.
Test random draw mode end-to-end on the production URL — draw a card, verify it renders.
Test Draw Three Keep One on production — draw three, select one, confirm interaction works.
Run Playwright e2e tests (`npm run test:e2e`) against the preview build if the test config supports a base URL override.
risks: The app uses hash-based routing which should work without server rewrites, but the acceptance criterion 'client-side routes resolve correctly after refresh' should be verified — the catch-all rewrite in vercel.json provides a safety net.
CSV data files in `Input/` or `public/` must be included in the build output; if they are fetched at runtime via relative paths, verify they resolve correctly on the production domain.
Vercel's auto-detection of Vite projects is generally reliable, but mismatched Node.js versions between local and Vercel could cause build failures — pin the Node version in vercel.json if needed.
No CI/CD pipeline exists — the first deployment is manual, which is fine for a release ticket but means future deploys need a deliberate trigger or a follow-up to add auto-deploy on push.
